preamble: In Japanese, the voiceless glottal fricative [h] undergoes allophonic variation depending on the following vowel. Before the close back compressed vowel /u/ [ɯ], it is realized as the voiceless bilabial fricative [ɸ].

examples.note: Standard Japanese pronunciation of syllables starting with 'f' (e.g., 'fuji' [ɸɯdʑi]).

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Tsujimura, N. (2013). An Introduction to Japanese Linguistics.
